title: identity.changed
description: Published by the Identity Service when admin database writes change a tenant or store record. Consumed by the mirror-sync-consumer to update identity_mirror tables in the data-platform Postgres so canonical FKs stay valid.

event_id.description: Unique identifier for this event. Used by the mirror-sync-consumer for idempotency.

event_type.description: What kind of change occurred. Deactivation is soft-delete; no hard-delete event exists.

entity_id.description: Internal UUID of the affected row: the tenant_id (for entity=tenant) or store_id (for entity=store). Identical to Customer Master core.tenants.id / core.stores.id.

tenant_id.description: Owning tenant UUID. For entity=tenant, same as entity_id. For entity=store, the parent tenant.

source_ts.description: Timestamp of the change in the admin database. Used as conflict-resolution key (older source_ts never overwrites newer).

payload.description: The current state of the entity. Shape depends on entity type. The mirror-sync-consumer projects only the fields that identity_mirror tables care about.

status.description: Lifecycle status replicated verbatim from Customer Master (tenants: e.g. ACTIVE/SUSPENDED/TERMINATED/ONBOARDING; stores: e.g. ACTIVE/INACTIVE/CLOSED). Replaces the prior is_active boolean; identity_mirror stores lifecycle as status, not is_active (decisions.md D46).

display_code.description: For entity=tenant: Customer Master's authoritative external code (tenants.display_code). Projected into identity_mirror.tenants.display_code by the mirror-sync-consumer.

store_code.description: For entity=store: Customer Master's authoritative external code (stores.store_code). Projected into identity_mirror.stores.store_code by the mirror-sync-consumer. Nullable at source.

metadata.description: Free-form additional fields. Receiver-side resolve methods may use; mirror-sync-consumer ignores.
